title: be going to (do)
desc: 계획된 의도 또는 근거 있는 예측
examples: I'm going to show you my routine while doing this challenge and along the way, you'll learn some new English vocabulary and expressions.
So, I'm going to head out for a walk.
Today I'm going to show you how I make my healthy and delicious smoothie.
But since it's almost gone, I'm going to dump it all.
I'm going to enjoy a big bowl of sujebi. Let's go.

title: will / shall
examples: I'm going to show you my routine while doing this challenge and along the way, you'll learn some new English vocabulary and expressions.
I think it'll be a good opportunity to build some healthy habits and get into better routines.
Sometimes, instead of going for a walk, I'll go for a run.
I'll make sure to share it with you guys.
On days where I have more energy, I'll grab my dumbbells and do some strength training.

title: can / could / be able to
examples: I always bring my headphones with me so I can listen to music or talk to my family on the phone.
Well, I'm out of breath and my heart is pounding, but I can't give up.
I can feel the burn.
Let's see how many pushups I can do.
You can choose whatever diet you want to do, but you can't have any cheat days for 75 days.

title: must / can't (deduction)
examples: And one of the workouts must be outside.
One rule of 75 hard is that one of my 45 minute workouts must be outside.
For 75 days I'm not eating gluten, I'm not eating sugar, and all of my meals must be cooked at home.

title: may / might (possibility)
examples: Walking may sound easy but when you're getting a minimum of 10k steps every single day, it's still pretty exhausting.
I might be a little bit rusty.

title: have to / must
examples: This challenge is called 75 hard and there are five rules that I must follow for 75 days straight.
Number two, I must work out every single day twice a day for 45 minutes each.
Rule number three, I must drink one gallon of water a day.
Rule number four, I must read 10 pages of non-fiction every single day.
The one bad thing about this part of the challenge is that you must work outside no matter what the weather is.

title: should (advice)
examples: So being sober for 75 days shouldn't be too much of a challenge, but I had a few events the past few weeks, like birthday parties, wedding parties, and that was really difficult.

title: Passive (is done / was done)
examples: This challenge is called 75 hard and there are five rules that I must follow for 75 days straight.
Also, this challenge is known as a mental toughness challenge.
I really love being out in nature and getting some fresh air.
For 75 days I'm not eating gluten, I'm not eating sugar, and all of my meals must be cooked at home.
I read The Cold Start Problem, Nonviolent Communication, Hood Feminism, Always Running, and my favorite book that I've read so far throughout this challenge is called Quiet by Susan Kane.

title: Passive (been done / being done)
examples: For 75 days I'm not eating gluten, I'm not eating sugar, and all of my meals must be cooked at home.

title: have something done
examples: I've already got my blender out.
